title: Khasianthus subsessilis
taxonomy: Khasianthus subsessilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Khasi Hills in Meghalaya, India. The classification of Khasianthus subsessilis is as follows: it belongs to the genus Khasianthus and the family Ericaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the heath family. The order to which this species belongs is Ericales, and the class is Magnoliopsida. This species is further classified as a dicotyledonous plant.
constituents: Khasianthus subsessilis contains bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The specific composition of active constituents in Khasianthus subsessilis may vary depending on the source and preparation of the plant. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's bioactive properties. Studies on Khasianthus subsessilis have shown potential therapeutic applications in traditional medicine.
parts: Khasianthus subsessilis is a plant species found in the Himalayas. Khasianthus subsessilis has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in India. The medicinal parts of Khasianthus subsessilis are primarily its leaves and roots. These parts are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Khasianthus subsessilis for medicinal purposes.
